growth_analysis.details: Revenue data incomplete; Positive FCFF growth; ROIC 61.9% (> 10 %)

risk_analysis.details: Beta NA; High D/E 1.4; Interest coverage NA

macro_context.market_sentiment: Bullish. S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit fresh intraday record highs on May 1 (+0.54% and +0.69% respectively), capping April as the best month for both indices since 2020. Apple's Q2 earnings beat was a key catalyst pushing the S&P 500 above 7,200.
macro_context.fed_outlook: Fed held rates steady at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29, 2026 in a contentious 8-4 vote (most dissents since October 1992). Third consecutive hold. Inflation remains elevated partly due to Middle East energy disruption. Kevin Warsh nominated to replace Jerome Powell as Fed chair. No near-term rate cuts signaled.
macro_context.geopolitical: US-Israel war on Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting global oil supply and raising energy prices; weighing on consumer spending outlook. US tariffs and export controls on semiconductors/critical minerals ongoing. US intervention in Venezuela and NATO posture in Greenland creating additional uncertainty.
key_events: Kevin Warsh Senate Banking Committee nomination advancing — potential Fed leadership shift
Strait of Hormuz closure continuing to pressure oil supply and inflation
Apple CEO transition: Tim Cook stepping down September 1, 2026; John Ternus takes over
Q3 FY2026 Apple earnings expected late July 2026
US tariff and export control regime ongoing — risk to Apple's China supply chain

ticker_news.headline: Apple Q2 FY2026 earnings beat: revenue $111.2B (+16.6% YoY), EPS $2.01 vs $1.94 estimate; gross margin 49.3% (record); stock +5%
Apple authorized additional $100 billion share buyback and raised dividend to $0.27/share (from $0.26), payable May 14
iPhone 17 is Apple's most popular iPhone line in company history, but supply constraints limited revenue; Services revenue reached all-time high
ticker_news.source: Apple Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript via Motley Fool

ticker_news.headline: Apple guided Q3 FY2026 revenue growth of +14% to +17% YoY, above analyst expectations
Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO on September 1, 2026; John Ternus (SVP Hardware Engineering) named successor

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: BNP Paribas raised PT to $300 (April 17, 2026). Wedbush (Dan Ives) PT $350, reiterated Outperform — most bullish on Street. Raymond James downgraded to Market Perform (neutral) at start of 2026 on AI lag concerns. Post-Q2 beat, several analysts cited better-than-feared results and strong guidance as catalysts. 77 analysts: 29 Buy, 16 Hold, 4 Sell; median PT $300, range $200-$350.

web_context.competitor_activity: Google's Gemini 3 AI model is outperforming Apple's native AI capabilities; Apple responded by announcing a partnership to use Google Gemini to power Siri — a significant defensive move. Microsoft continues embedding AI broadly into its product ecosystem, maintaining competitive pressure on Apple's enterprise and productivity segments. Samsung competes directly across smartphones, tablets, and wearables, though Apple overtook Samsung in global smartphone shipments for the first time in 14 years in 2025. Apple's AI lag vs. Google/Microsoft was the primary overhang on AAPL stock YTD before the Q2 earnings beat.
web_context.earnings_info: Q2 FY2026 (Jan-Mar 2026) reported April 30, 2026: EPS $2.01 beat estimate $1.94 by 3.6%; revenue $111.2B beat estimates. Next earnings: Q3 FY2026 (Apr-Jun 2026) expected late July 2026 (no formal date announced yet). Company guidance: Q3 revenue +14% to +17% YoY. Street consensus EPS estimate for Q3 not yet finalized post-Q2 call.
